title: Globally defined SV function breaks CXXRTL export
body:
### Minimal Working Example:
```systemverilog
//top.sv
function int f(input int p);
    f = p;
endfunction

module top(input int i, output int o);
mod m(
    .i(i),
    .o(o)
);
endmodule

(* cxxrtl_blackbox *)
module mod(input int i, output int o);
endmodule
```
```
read_verilog -sv top.sv
write_cxxrtl
```
### Expected result:
c++ code with `struct bb_p_mod` and `struct p_top`

### Actual Result:
c++ code with only `struct p_top`, which is empty

### Details:
Existence of the function f() ---although not used--- causes `write_cxxrtl` to produce incomplete output.
Removing `f()` or moving it into `top` or to the end of the file produces expect output.
